response:
Wade buys three sandwiches and two drinks at the rest stop for his journey. The drinks cost four dollars each which totals eight dollars for the beverages. He spent twenty-six dollars in total so the sandwiches must cost eighteen dollars combined. Each sandwich therefore costs six dollars when the total is divided by the number of items bought in the lab.

***

The calculation shows that the cost per sandwich is six dollars based on the total spent. Wade can now enjoy his food while traveling along the road with [money] and [time] to spare. He plans to eat one now and save the others for later at [location] or [destination]. This meal will keep him full until he reaches his final [goal].
